front: Print: woodblock series depicting stations along the Tokaido road from Edo to Kyoto, beginning at Nihonbashi Bridge
back: The Fifty-Three Stations of the Tokaido (Utagawa Hiroshige)
work: The Fifty-Three Stations of the Tokaido (1833–1834)

front: Print: thin diagonal black lines represent driving rain over a bridge as figures cower under umbrellas
back: Sudden Shower over Shin-Ohashi Bridge (Utagawa Hiroshige)
work: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo (1856–1858)

front: Print: Van Gogh copied it as Bridge in the Rain and added faux calligraphy around the border
back: Sudden Shower over Shin-Ohashi Bridge (Utagawa Hiroshige)
work: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo (1856–1858)

back: Sudden Shower over Shin-Ohashi Bridge (Utagawa Hiroshige)
work: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo (1856–1858)

front: Print: portrait-oriented woodblock showing abstracted 'Sleeping Dragon' plum tree branches against a deep red sky at Kameido
work: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo (1856–1858)

front: Print: its red hue and prominent horizontal branch inspired Gauguin's Vision After the Sermon
work: One Hundred Famous Views of Edo (1856–1858)

front: Artist: last great ukiyo-e master of the Utagawa school
Artist: also created a 69-station woodblock print series depicting the Kiso Kaido road
Artist: Szymborska's poetry collection The People on the Bridge was inspired by one of his woodblock prints
